metadata.author: Jeremiah
metadata.subject: Jehovah's Expression of His Love for the Chosen People through His Chastisement of Them in His Dealing with the Nations.

1: The words of Jeremiah the son of Hilkiah, of the priests who were in Anathoth in the land of Benjamin,
To whom the word of Jehovah came in the days of Josiah the son of Amon, king of Judah, in the thirteenth year of his reign.
Before I formed you in the womb, I knew you; and before you came forth from the womb, I sanctified you; I appointed you a prophet to the nations.
31: Behold, the days are coming, declares Jehovah, when I will make a new covenant with the house of Israel and with the house of Judah,
Not like the covenant which I made with their fathers in the day that I took them by the hand to bring them out of the land of Egypt...
I will put My law within them, and upon their hearts I will write it; and I will be their God, and they will be My people.

5.text: God's 'knowing' Jeremiah before birth signifies His sovereign selection and predestination. This applies to all believers who were chosen in Christ before the foundation of the world.

33.text: The New Covenant is characterized by an inward law of life rather than an outward law of letters. This is the basis of our organic union with God in the New Testament.
